title: Pyramid of Hawara
description: The mud-brick pyramid of Amenemhat III, known as the "Black Pyramid", located at the entrance to the Faiyum oasis. Era: Middle Kingdom (12th Dynasty).

title: The Egyptian Labyrinth
description: The scant remains of the legendary mortuary temple of Amenemhat III at Hawara, once described by Herodotus as surpassing the pyramids. Area: Hawara. Era: Middle Kingdom.

title: Pyramid of Lahun
description: Mud-brick pyramid of Senusret II, famous for the discovery of the "Treasure of Lahun" and the nearby worker's village. Area: Lahun. Era: Middle Kingdom (12th Dynasty).

title: Pyramid of Seila
description: A small step pyramid built by Sneferu on the eastern edge of the Faiyum, overlooking the cultivation. Area: Seila. Era: Old Kingdom (4th Dynasty).

title: Karanis (Kom Aushim)
description: One of the largest and best-preserved Greco-Roman cities in Egypt, featuring temples to Sobek and domestic mud-brick architecture. Area: Kom Aushim. Era: Ptolemaic/Roman.

title: Dimeh el-Siba (Soknopaiou Nesos)
description: A well-preserved Greco-Roman town north of Lake Qarun, with a prominent paved processional way and temple to Sobek. Area: North Faiyum. Era: Ptolemaic/Roman.

title: Qasr Qarun (Dionysias)
description: The ancient city of Dionysias, featuring a remarkably intact late Ptolemaic temple dedicated to Sobek-Ra. Area: West Faiyum. Era: Ptolemaic/Roman.

title: Medinet Maadi (Narmouthis)
description: Features a Middle Kingdom temple dedicated to Renenutet and Sobek, one of the few surviving from that era, plus Ptolemaic expansions. Area: Southwest Faiyum. Era: Middle Kingdom.

title: Qasr al-Sagha
description: A mysterious, unfinished limestone temple with seven chapels, located deep in the desert north of the lake. Area: North Faiyum. Era: Middle Kingdom.

title: Batn Ihrit (Theadelphia)
description: Ruins of the Greco-Roman town of Theadelphia, known for papyri finds and a temple to Pnepheros. Area: West Faiyum. Era: Ptolemaic/Roman.

title: Philadelphia (Kom el-Kharaba)
description: A Model town established by Ptolemy II Philadelphus, laid out on a grid plan. Area: Northeast Faiyum. Era: Ptolemaic.

title: Tebtunis (Umm el-Baragat)
description: A major cult center for Sobek in the south of Faiyum, famous for crocodile mummies and extensive papyri texts. Area: South Faiyum. Era: Ptolemaic/Roman.

title: Umm al-Athl (Bacchias)
description: The ruins of Bacchias, a temple town dedicated to Soknobraisis, near the eastern entrance of the oasis. Area: Northeast Faiyum. Era: Ptolemaic/Roman.

title: Qasr El-Banat (Euhemeria)
description: Ruins of Euhemeria, a town founded by Ptolemy II, known for its temple and Roman-era bathhouse. Area: West Faiyum. Era: Ptolemaic/Roman.

title: Philoteris (Watfa)
description: A settlement established by Ptolemy II, named after his sister Philotera. Area: West Faiyum. Era: Ptolemaic.

title: Kom el-Atl
description: A less explored archaeological mound likely containing remains of a Greco-Roman settlement. Area: Northeast Faiyum. Era: Roman.

title: Deir el-Azab (Monastery of the Virgin)
description: An ancient Coptic monastery south of Faiyum City, known for the relics of St. Abraam. Area: Azab. Era: Roman/Coptic.

title: Deir al-Malak (Monastery of Archangel Gabriel)
description: Located in the Naqlun mountains, known for its caves where early anchorites lived. Area: Naqlun. Era: Coptic/Byzantine.

title: Hanging Mosque (Mosque of Khawand Aslabay)
description: A unique Mamluk-era mosque built on high ground (hence "Hanging"), commissioned by the wife of Sultan Qaitbay. Area: Fayoum City. Era: Mamluk (1476 AD).

title: Mosque of Qaitbay
description: A historic mosque built by the Mamluk Sultan Qaitbay, featuring classic Mamluk architecture. Area: Fayoum City. Era: Mamluk.

title: Mosque of Sheikh Ali al-Roubi
description: The shrine and mosque of the local Sufi saint Ali al-Roubi, a significant pilgrimage site. Area: Fayoum City. Era: Mamluk/Ottoman.

title: Lahun Regulator (Barrage)
description: Historic waterworks regulating the flow of the Bahr Yussef into the Faiyum depression. Area: Lahun. Era: Pharaonic/Islamic renovations.

title: Biahmou Colossi Bases
description: Two massive limestone pedestals that once supported colossal seated statues of Amenemhat III. Area: Biahmou. Era: Middle Kingdom.

title: Obelisk of Senusret I (Abgig Obelisk)
description: A unique red granite obelisk with a rounded top, originally from Abgig, now in a traffic circle. Area: Fayoum City. Era: Middle Kingdom.

title: Wadi El Hitan (Whale Valley)
description: UNESCO World Heritage site featuring fossilized skeletons of early whales (Archaeoceti). Area: Western Desert. Era: Eocene (Natural Heritage).

title: Gebel Qatrani Open Air Museum
description: Site rich in fossils and petrified wood, north of Lake Qarun. Area: North Faiyum. Era: Oligocene.

title: Widan el-Faras Basalt Quarries
description: Ancient Old Kingdom basalt quarries used for pyramid temple paving stones. Area: Gebel Qatrani. Era: Old Kingdom.

title: Lake Moeris Quarry Road
description: Considered the world's oldest paved road, used to transport basalt blocks to the lake. Area: North Faiyum. Era: Old Kingdom.

title: Kom Talit (Talia)
description: Ruins of a Roman-era town in the south of the depression. Area: South Faiyum. Era: Roman.

title: Kom Ruqayya
description: archaeological site south of the lake, containing remains of settlements and cemeteries. Area: South Faiyum. Era: Greco-Roman.

title: Medinet Gurob
description: New Kingdom palace town and harem established by Thutmose III. Area: Gurob. Era: New Kingdom.

title: Kom el-Hammam
description: Site near Lahun containing Roman period remains. Area: Southeast Faiyum. Era: Roman.

title: Deir el-Hammam
description: A Coptic monastery located near the Lahun pyramid. Area: Lahun. Era: Coptic.

title: Ain El-Siliyin
description: Historic natural springs and gardens, a traditional recreational spot in Faiyum. Area: Siliyin. Era: Traditional.

title: Water Wheels of Faiyum
description: Iconic hydraulic engineering symbols of Faiyum, used since Ptolemaic times for irrigation. Area: Fayoum City. Era: Ptolemaic/Islamic.

title: Kom Danial
description: Archaeological mound with layers from the Greco-Roman period. Area: Central Faiyum. Era: Greco-Roman.

title: Qasr Rashwan
description: Remains of a Roman villa or settlement on the northeastern edge. Area: Northeast Faiyum. Era: Roman.

title: Kom el-Asl (Magdola)
description: Site of the ancient large village of Magdola, featuring broad Ptolemaic housing. Area: South Faiyum. Era: Ptolemaic.

title: Gerza
description: Predynastic and Early Dynastic cemetery site, giving name to the Gerzean culture. Area: Gerza. Era: Predynastic.

title: Sedment El-Gebel
description: Necropolis serving Herakleopolis Magna and nearby Faiyum entrance area. Area: Sedment. Era: Pharaonic.

title: Wadi El Rayan
description: Protected area with upper and lower lakes, waterfalls, and fossil deposits. Area: Southwest Faiyum. Era: Natural.

title: Harageh
description: Site of Middle Kingdom cemeteries east of Lahun. Area: Harageh. Era: Middle Kingdom.

title: Dimai
description: The surrounding landscape and processional way leading to the temple at Soknopaiou Nesos. Area: North Faiyum. Era: Greco-Roman.

title: Kom Aushim Museum
description: Small museum housing artifacts found at Karanis and other Faiyum sites. Area: Kom Aushim. Era: Modern.

title: Magic Lake
description: A beautiful lake surrounded by sand dunes, overlooking Wadi El Rayan. Area: Wadi El Rayan. Era: Natural.
